Meeting Cost Calculator

Calculate the true dollar cost of meetings based on attendee count, hourly rates, meeting length, and frequency.

$
$
$
$

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter the number of people who attend this meeting.
2
Enter the average fully-loaded hourly rate per attendee.
3
Enter the typical length of this meeting in minutes.
4
Enter how many times per week this meeting occurs.
5
Review the per-meeting, weekly, and annual cost in both dollars and hours.
6
Scroll down to compare costs across 9 different meeting durations.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I determine the average hourly rate for attendees?

For employees, use fully-loaded cost (salary + benefits + overhead divided by 2,080 hours). For freelancers and entrepreneurs, use your billable rate or opportunity cost. A good rule of thumb is $50-$150/hr depending on role seniority. Use $75/hr as a reasonable default for knowledge workers.

What is the biggest hidden cost of meetings?

Context switching. A 30-minute meeting typically costs 15-25 additional minutes of lost productivity before and after as people mentally shift gears. For knowledge workers, this can effectively double the true cost. For deep work requiring 60-90 minutes of uninterrupted focus, even a short meeting can destroy half a day of meaningful output.

How can I reduce meeting costs without eliminating collaboration?

Try async standups (written updates in Slack or Notion), Loom video recordings for presentations, decision documents with async comment periods, office hours instead of scheduled meetings, and reducing default meeting length (15 min instead of 30, 25 min instead of 60). Remove non-decision-makers from recurring meetings.

Are there meetings that are worth the cost?

Yes. Decision-making meetings with clear agendas and the right decision-makers are worth the cost if they prevent weeks of misaligned work. 1-on-1s between managers and direct reports (30 min/week) have high ROI for alignment and retention. Brainstorming sessions with a facilitator and clear output goals can generate outsized value.

How do I calculate the opportunity cost of a meeting?

Multiply the meeting cost by 1.5-2x to account for context-switching overhead. Then compare against what attendees could have produced in that time. If a $225 meeting could have generated $2,000 in billable work, the opportunity cost is significant. This is why senior engineers and executives should attend the fewest meetings.

Results

Example

⏰ Meeting Cost Calculator

📋 Meeting Setup • Attendees: 6 people • Avg Hourly Rate: $75/hr • Meeting Length: 30 min (0.50 hrs) • Meetings Per Week: 1 💸 True Cost of Meetings • Cost Per Meeting: $225.00 • Weekly Cost: $225.00 (3.00 person-hours) • Annual Cost: $10,800.00 (144 person-hours) 📊 Moderate. $225/week on meetings is typical. Audit recurring meetings quarterly — are they all still necessary?
